mission.title: argo-workflows: Kubernetes 1.24: Token-handling code assumes auto-created service account token secrets
mission.description: Kubernetes 1.24: Token-handling code assumes auto-created service account token secrets. This issue affects 38+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-workflows troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-workflows deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows -n argo-workflows --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-workflows configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-workflows configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get configmap -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-workflows
```
Starting with Kubernetes 1.24, auto-generated service account token secrets are no longer created. Argo Workflows code that assumes these tokens exist will fail.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Kubernetes 1.24: Token-handling code assumes auto-created…
steps.description:
Update Argo Workflows to handle Kubernetes 1.24+ token changes. Instead of relying on auto-created token secrets, use projected volumes or manually create a service account token Secret:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: argo-workflow-sa
  namespace: argo-workflows
secrets:
- name: argo-workflow-sa-token
```

steps.title: Confirm Kubernetes 1.24: Token-handling code assumes… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows -n argo-workflows --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-workflows --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Updated token-handling code to not assume auto-created service account token secrets exist. For Kubernetes 1.24+, manually create a service account token Secret and reference it in the ServiceAccount spec.
codeSnippets:
git reset HEAD~1 ;# change 1 to how many commits you made
git commit --signoff -m 'feat: my feat. Fixes #1234'

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
.......
secrets:
- name: the-secret-token-name

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
